LAW AND MOTION 302,1 - DEFENDANTS RYAN-JAMES WARMAN , PH.D., APPEARING AS BENEFICIARY, AGENT, AND AUTHORIZED REPRESENTATIVE FOR: RYAN J WARMAN, PRINCIPAL AND REPRESENTED PERSON MOTION TO DISMISS. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO DISMISS IS DENIED. BECAUSE THE STATED GROUNDS FOR THE MOTION ARE, AMONG OTHER THINGS, PLAINTIFF CITIBANK'S SOLE CAUSE OF ACTION FAILS TO STATE A CLAIM, THE COURT HAS TREATED THE MOTION AS A DEMURRER. THE MOTION FAILS TO SHOW ANY DEFECT IN THE COMPLAINT THAT WARRANTS SUSTAINING A DEMURRER. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/HEK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, 2 - DEFENDANT RYAN-JAMES WARMAN , PH.D., APPEARING AS BENEFICIARY, AGENT, AND AUTHORIZED REPRESENTATIVE FOR: RYAN J WARMAN, PRINCIPAL AND REPRESENTED PERSON'S MOTION TO SEAL UNREDACTED DOCUMENTS PURSUANT TO CRC 2.550-2.551. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO SEAL IS DENIED. THE MOTION FAILS TO SHOW ANY OVERRIDING INTEREST, MUCH LESS THAT AN OVERRIDING INTEREST WOULD BE PREJUDICED BY PUBLIC DISCLOSURE OF THE PAPERS FILED IN THIS CASE SEEKING PAYMENT ON A CREDIT CARD DEBT. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/HEK)

LAW AND MOTION 302, 3 - DEFENDANTS RYAN-JAMES WARMAN , PH.D., APPEARING AS BENEFICIARY, AGENT, AND AUTHORIZED REPRESENTATIVE FOR: RYAN J WARMAN, PRINCIPAL AND REPRESENTED PERSON NOTICE OF DEFAULT AND REQUEST FOR DECLARATORY AND INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. DEFENDANT'S NOTICE OF DEFAULT AND REQUEST FOR DECLARATORY AND RELIEF IS DENIED. THE MOTION APPEARS TO BE SEEKING TEMPORARY, PRELIMINARY AND/OR PERMANENT INJUNCTIVE RELIEF AND/OR AN IMMEDIATE DECLARATION, BUT FAILS TO PROVIDE ANY COMPETENT EVIDENCE SHOWING ENTITLEMENT TO SUCH RELIEF. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING APPROVED AS TO FORM. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/HEK)

MOTION TO STRIKE DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-09-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

2 - Defendant and Cross-Complainant Ryan J Warman's Motion to Strike Plaintiff and Cross-Defendant Citibank, N.A.'s Demurrer to the Cross-Complaint is GRANTED. Cross-defendant shall answer by October 30, 2025.

On July 23, 2025, the court ordered cross-defendant to file a response to the cross-complaint by August 25, 2025. Cross-defendant filed this untimely demurrer on August 29, 2025. Moreover, the demurrer is not well presented. Cross-defendant references UCC Financing Statements and Secretary of State documents, but fails to provide them. Cross-defendant also failed to make a request for judicial notice per Evidence Code section 452. Cross-defendant likely relies on the Secretary of State documents it provided on May 28, 2025. Those documents, however, do not establish that cross-complainant's claims fail as a matter of law. On the other hand, the court notes the cross-complaint is unclear as to what it is actually alleging.

The court orders: (1) Cross-complainant must review his cross complaint; (2) the parties must meet and confer and explore the deficiencies raised by Cross-defendant; (3) Cross-complainant must seriously consider amending the cross-complaint to clarify his claims; (4) if cross-complainant elects to amend, he has leave to do so no later than November 14, 2025; and (5) Cross-defendant shall not file any new motion challenging the cross-complaint before November 17, 2025.

Order is signed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

Motion For Leave To File Counterclaim
Off Calendar Mar-18-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Jason Burrows, Esq. 800-506-2652 The Moore Law Group for Plaintiff.
Ryan Warman, 415-340-9924 Defendant in Pro Per. (In-person)

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT RYAN-JAMES WARMAN , PH.D., APPEARING AS BENEFICIARY, AGENT, AND AUTHORIZED REPRESENTATIVE FOR: RYAN J WARMAN's Motion For Leave To File Counterclaim.

Defendant Ryan-James Warman's motion for leave to file a cross-complaint (erroneously referred to in the motion papers as a counterclaim) is granted. Mr. Warman must file and serve his cross-complaint no later than April 2, 2025. California has a strong policy in favor of allowing the filing of pleadings so that cases may be tried on their merits. Plaintiff Citibank has not shown that it would suffer legal prejudice by the granting of this motion. Citibank's arguments regarding the asserted lack of merit to the cross-complaint are better addressed by a pleading motion such as a demurrer or an evidentiary motion such as a motion for summary judgment rather than on a motion to amend, which is particularly true in this case since Mr. Warman's moving papers do not include a copy of his proposed cross-complaint.

Order is signed.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/HEK)
